homeTitle: AI-Based Prediction of Pile-Induced Construction Noise
homeDescription: Machine learning-based framework for predicting pile-induced construction noise using field measurements, construction activity data, soil parameters, and digital visualization.
detailTitle: AI-Based Prediction of Pile-Induced Construction Noise
detailSubtitle: Machine Learning-Based Noise Prediction and Screening Framework
detailDescription: This research focuses on predicting construction noise generated during pile construction activities using field measurements, activity information, soil parameters, distance, elapsed time, and machine learning models.

The study aims to support construction noise assessment, environmental risk identification, buffer zone planning, and smart construction decision-making. The framework combines noise measurement, data preprocessing, machine learning model development, performance evaluation, and web-based visualization.

The final outcome supports engineers, researchers, and construction stakeholders by providing a digital tool for predicting noise levels and identifying high-noise risk zones around construction sites.
tools: Python, Machine Learning, Flask, Google Colab, VS Code, Data Visualization
researchAreas: Construction Noise, Environmental Engineering, Artificial Intelligence, Smart Construction, Digital Twin
futureDirection: BIM-integrated digital twin platform for construction environmental risk prediction and real-time noise management.
